GENESIS workshop 2026 2026-07-21 1 Introduction This site contains course materials for Bioconductor Africa: GENESIS workshop on July 22 and 29, 2026. Data used is located in the github repository from which the site is built. Slides for lectures are linked below in the schedule. Background material (not covered in workshop): GWAS crash course Variant annotation: (slides) (video) the SISG 2020 “Computational Pipeline for WGS Data” class with videos: https://uw-gac.github.io/SISG_2020 1.1 Schedule Wednesday, July 22 Introduction and Genomic Data Storage Intro to Genomic Data Storage (slides) Demo - GDS format in R Association tests GENESIS for association tests (slides) Demo - Association tests in R Population Structure and Relatedness R packages for PCA and relatedness (slides) Demo - Population structure and relatedness in R Wednesday, July 29 Mixed models Demo - Mixed models in R Variant annotation Variant annotation (slides) Demo - Using variant annotation Aggregate tests Demo - Aggregate tests Download the workshop data and exercises: https://github.com/smgogarten/GENESIS_workshop_2026/archive/refs/heads/main.zip 1.2 R packages used SeqArray SeqVarTools GENESIS SNPRelate tidyverse GGally 1.3 Resources If you are new to R, you might find the following material helpful: Introduction to R Graphics with ggplot2 tutorial Data manipulation with dplyr If you have access to a HPC cluster or want more information on running GENESIS functions in parallel on your computer, read about the BiocParallel package. Each GENESIS function takes a BiocParallel parameter object as an optional input, which you can customize to your computing setup.
